questions.prompt: What is the best sign that a download deserves extra caution?
options: It promises free rewards and asks you to disable security settings
It comes from an official app store or vendor page
It has clear reviews from known sources
It explains what permissions it needs
questions.explanation: Requests to disable protections are a serious warning sign. Topic focus: Password Power.

questions.prompt: A public Wi-Fi network at a cafe has no password. Which action is safest?
options: Use it for banking because it is convenient
Avoid sensitive tasks or use a trusted hotspot or VPN
Turn off updates to save data
Share the network password with everyone nearby
questions.explanation: Open networks are easier to monitor. Sensitive tasks should wait or use a safer connection. Topic focus: Password Power.

questions.prompt: A quiz asks for your full name, school, birthday, and home address to reveal your 'cyber hero name.' What should you do?
options: Enter everything so the result is accurate
Give only the information you are comfortable sharing or skip it
Use a parent's payment card instead
Send the quiz to younger students
questions.explanation: Fun quizzes can collect personal data. You can decline or minimize what you share. Topic focus: Password Power.

questions.prompt: Which message is most likely to be a scam?
options: Your teacher posts homework inside the official classroom app
A bank text asks you to move money to a 'safe account' right now
A parent reminds you to update your device tonight
A known store receipt arrives after a purchase you made
questions.explanation: Pressure to move money quickly is a classic scam pattern. Topic focus: Password Power.

questions.prompt: What should you do after clicking a suspicious link by mistake?
options: Ignore it if nothing happens right away
Enter fake details to test the page
Close it, do not enter information, and report it to a trusted person
Share the link so others can inspect it
questions.explanation: Fast reporting and avoiding data entry reduce the risk. Topic focus: Password Power.

questions.prompt: An app requests access to your contacts, camera, microphone, and location for a simple puzzle game. What is a safe response?
options: Accept all permissions because games need everything
Deny permissions that do not match the app's purpose
Give it your password instead
Turn off the lock screen
questions.explanation: Permissions should fit the app's real function. Topic focus: Password Power.

questions.prompt: Which action helps a family recover faster after an account breach?
options: Keep recovery email and phone details current
Delete all messages without checking them
Use one shared password for every account
Avoid telling anyone
questions.explanation: Current recovery details make it easier to regain control safely. Topic focus: Password Power.

questions.prompt: A caller says they are from tech support and asks to remote-control your device. What should you do?
options: Give control if they sound professional
Hang up and contact the company using its official support page
Read out all saved passwords
Install whatever they request
questions.explanation: Unexpected support calls are risky. Start a new conversation through official channels. Topic focus: Password Power.

questions.prompt: What makes multi-factor authentication useful?

It adds another proof step if a password is stolen
It lets friends log in for you

questions.explanation: Fast reporting and avoiding data entry reduce the risk. Topic focus: Password Power.

questions.prompt: A message says your account will be closed in 10 minutes unless you sign in through a link. What is the safest first move?
options: Open the link immediately so the account stays active
Use the official app or website directly and check alerts there
Reply with your password so support can verify you
Forward the message to friends to ask if it is real
questions.explanation: Urgent account warnings are common phishing hooks. Going directly to the official service avoids a fake link. Topic focus: Password Power.

questions.prompt: Which password habit is strongest for a school, game, or shopping account?
options: Use the same password everywhere so it is easy to remember
Use a unique long password or passphrase for each important account
Use your pet's name and birth year
Share your password only with close friends
questions.explanation: Unique passwords limit damage if one account is exposed. Topic focus: Password Power.

questions.prompt: A friend asks for a login code that was texted to your phone. What should you do?
options: Send the code because friends are safe
Post the code in the group chat
Do not share the code and contact the friend another way
Try the code on several websites
questions.explanation: Login codes are account keys. A request for one may mean the friend's account was taken over. Topic focus: Password Power.

Any form with a logo is trustworthy
Payment requests are safe when they are small
questions.explanation: Multiple risk signals together mean you should verify independently before sharing anything. Topic focus: Password Power.

questions.prompt: Which password habit is strongest for a school, game, or shopping account?
options: Use the same password everywhere so it is easy to remember
Use a unique long password or passphrase for each important account
Use your pet's name and birth year
Share your password only with close friends
questions.explanation: Unique passwords limit damage if one account is exposed. Topic focus: Password Power.

questions.prompt: What should you do after clicking a suspicious link by mistake?
options: Ignore it if nothing happens right away
Enter fake details to test the page
Close it, do not enter information, and report it to a trusted person
Share the link so others can inspect it
questions.explanation: Fast reporting and avoiding data entry reduce the risk. Topic focus: Password Power.

questions.prompt: An app requests access to your contacts, camera, microphone, and location for a simple puzzle game. What is a safe response?
options: Accept all permissions because games need everything
Deny permissions that do not match the app's purpose
Give it your password instead
Turn off the lock screen
questions.explanation: Permissions should fit the app's real function. Topic focus: Password Power.

questions.prompt: Which action helps a family recover faster after an account breach?
options: Keep recovery email and phone details current
Delete all messages without checking them
Use one shared password for every account
Avoid telling anyone
questions.explanation: Current recovery details make it easier to regain control safely. Topic focus: Password Power.

questions.prompt: A caller says they are from tech support and asks to remote-control your device. What should you do?
options: Give control if they sound professional
Hang up and contact the company using its official support page
Read out all saved passwords
Install whatever they request
questions.explanation: Unexpected support calls are risky. Start a new conversation through official channels. Topic focus: Password Power.

questions.prompt: What makes multi-factor authentication useful?
options: It makes passwords unnecessary everywhere
It adds another proof step if a password is stolen
It lets friends log in for you
It hides all online activity
questions.explanation: MFA helps block sign-ins that only have the password. Topic focus: Password Power.

questions.prompt: A message says your account will be closed in 10 minutes unless you sign in through a link. What is the safest first move?
options: Open the link immediately so the account stays active
Use the official app or website directly and check alerts there
Reply with your password so support can verify you
Forward the message to friends to ask if it is real
questions.explanation: Urgent account warnings are common phishing hooks. Going directly to the official service avoids a fake link. Topic focus: Password Power.

questions.prompt: Which password habit is strongest for a school, game, or shopping account?
options: Use the same password everywhere so it is easy to remember
Use a unique long password or passphrase for each important account
Use your pet's name and birth year
Share your password only with close friends
questions.explanation: Unique passwords limit damage if one account is exposed. Topic focus: Password Power.

questions.prompt: A friend asks for a login code that was texted to your phone. What should you do?
options: Send the code because friends are safe
Post the code in the group chat
Do not share the code and contact the friend another way
Try the code on several websites
questions.explanation: Login codes are account keys. A request for one may mean the friend's account was taken over. Topic focus: Password Power.
